name: batch_read
description: Read multiple files in one call with inline ranges, --lines, --head/--tail, --number, --count, --show-all, --skip-empty, REPEATABLE --grep patterns with --context/-A/-B/-C, --start-from=N, and --func=NAME/--from-marker=REGEX/--to-marker=REGEX to read function/marker-delimited blocks.

parameters.name: --dir=DIR
parameters.description: Working directory to cd into before reading

parameters.name: files
parameters.description: Files to read; use file:START-END for inline line range(s)

parameters.name: --start-from=N
parameters.description: Show from line N to end of file (equivalent to --lines=N-)

parameters.name: --lines=START-END[,START2-END2...]
parameters.description: Line range(s) like 10-30, 200- (open-ended), or 10-30,50-70; supports combining with --head/--tail to truncate output

parameters.name: --show-all,-A
parameters.description: Show all characters (cat -A) to reveal special/escape chars

parameters.name: --grep=PATTERN (repeatable)
parameters.description: Show lines matching pattern (repeatable); scoped to --lines or inline ranges; supports --ignore-case, --after-context/-A, --before-context/-B, --context/-C

parameters.name: --after-context=N, -A=N
parameters.description: Show N lines after each grep match (like grep -A)

parameters.name: --before-context=N, -B=N
parameters.description: Show N lines before each grep match (like grep -B)

parameters.name: --context=N, -C=N
parameters.description: Show N lines before and after each grep match (like grep -C)

parameters.name: --func=NAME
parameters.description: Read function body from func NAME to next func declaration

parameters.name: --from-marker=REGEX
parameters.description: Start reading from first line matching REGEX

parameters.name: --to-marker=REGEX
parameters.description: Stop reading before first line matching REGEX (after from-marker)

examples.expected: Shows the full body of evalArrayIndexExpression up to the next function

cost_saving_rationale: Collapses N separate cat/sed/wc/grep calls into 1 step; --func replaces sed -n /^func X/,/^func Y/p patterns.
